elements.sent-2411-001: We study forest-skein (FS) groups using dynamics.
elements.sent-2411-002: A simple Ore FS category produces three FS groups analogous to Richard Thompson's groups.
elements.sent-2411-003: Reconstruction theorems of McCleary and Rubin apply to these FS groups: each of them encodes a canonical rigid group action and thus carries powerful dynamical invariants.
elements.sent-2411-004: We then explicitly construct infinitely many isomorphism classes of finitely presented (of type $\mathrm{F _\infty$) infinite simple groups which act faithfully on the circle by (orientation-preserving) homeomorphisms, but admit no non-trivial finite piecewise linear actions nor finite piecewise projective actions.}
elements.sent-2411-005: To the best of our knowledge these are the first examples witnessing these properties.
elements.sent-2411-006: We also show these groups fit into the finite germ extension framework of Belk, Hyde, and Matucci.
elements.sent-2411-007: Inspired by deep connections uncovered by Vaughan Jones between Richard Thompson's group $F,T,V$, subfactors, and conformal field theory, the first author has introduced forest-skein (FS) groups --- a class of ``Thompson-like'' groups which mix features of $F,T,V$ with Jones' planar algebra \cite{Jones17,Brothier20,Brothier22 .}
elements.sent-2411-008: This article uses the dynamics of FS groups to construct new examples of finitely presented simple groups, classifying them, and witnessing novel properties.
elements.sent-2411-009: To any set $S$ of colours and set $R$ of skein relations over $S$ (pair of binary trees with same number of leaves having interior vertices labelled by $S$) one may consider the FS category $\cF$ with skein presentation $\la S|R\ra$.
elements.sent-2411-010: This consists of all forests with interior vertices coloured by $S$ modulo the skein relations in $R$.
elements.sent-2411-011: Often $\cF$ admits a calculus of fractions, meaning we can build groups $G^F\subset G^T\subset G^V$ of tree-pair diagrams from $\cF$ similar to $F\subset T\subset V$, the latter corresponding to skein presentation $\la a|\varnothing\ra$.
